factory.crossbow.description: "The arrow went through all the axes without touching earth." — Homer, The Odyssey
factory.crossbow.manager.description: Marksman of the Alpine passes; his guild drills until the bolt finds apple or tyrant alike.
factory.crossbow.manager.name: William Tell
factory.crossbow.name: Crossbow
factory.crossbow.upgrade.description: Counterweight engines and siege crews; walls crumble before the charge, and ransom follows every breach.
factory.crossbow.upgrade.name: Trebuchets
factory.grain.description: "In the eyes of the hungry there is a growing wrath." — John Steinbeck, The Grapes of Wrath
factory.grain.manager.description: Greek mistress of the threshing floor; every bound sheaf in Eleusis feeds law and lance alike.
factory.grain.manager.name: Demeter
factory.grain.name: Grain
factory.grain.upgrade.description: Channels and royal silos tied to the river; famine rarely returns.
factory.grain.upgrade.name: Irrigation
factory.iron.description: "The eternal smith forged and hammered without ceasing." — Elias Lönnrot, The Kalevala
factory.iron.manager.description: Eternal smith of the Kalevala; his forge never cools and every ingot rings for plough and blade.
factory.iron.manager.name: Ilmarinen
factory.iron.name: Iron
factory.iron.upgrade.description: Smiths turn ingot into blade, mail, buckle, and ploughshare; every levy marches equipped and every field is shod in iron.
factory.iron.upgrade.name: Armories
factory.longship.description: "I take to the sea to drive off the spleen." — Herman Melville, Moby-Dick
factory.longship.manager.description: Mariner of Basra and the seven seas; every hull he sends widens the crown upon the water.
factory.longship.manager.name: Sinbad
factory.longship.name: Longship
factory.longship.upgrade.description: Chartered companies plant colonies and carry spice, wool, and silver; every voyage widens the crown's purse.
factory.longship.upgrade.name: Colonial Trade
factory.wine.description: "Gilgamesh, where are you hurrying to? You will never find the life for which you are looking." — Epic of Gilgamesh
factory.wine.manager.description: Keeper of the vine at the edge of the sea; her tavern turns grape into liquid gold faster than any vine alone.
factory.wine.manager.name: Siduri
factory.wine.name: Wine
factory.wine.upgrade.description: Charter taverns at every crossroad and harbor; pilgrims, merchants, and weary kings pay well for a cup and a night's rest.
factory.wine.upgrade.name: Taverns
factory.reliquary.description: "And then Sir Launcelot saw the Holy Grail covered with white samite." — Thomas Malory, Le Morte d'Arthur
factory.reliquary.manager.description: Martyr of Canterbury; pilgrims flock to his shrine with wax-sealed gifts and silver awe.
factory.reliquary.manager.name: Thomas Becket
factory.reliquary.name: Reliquary
factory.reliquary.upgrade.description: Every hearth pays the tithe, every child learns the creed, and pilgrim offerings swell the treasury—faith becomes coin by law.
factory.reliquary.upgrade.name: Tithes
god.dagda.description: Call the Dagda of Ireland; his cauldron and harp fatten every wheel along the river.
god.dagda.name: Dagda
god.huangdi.description: Invoke the Yellow Emperor of China; the Mandate roots harvest and dynasty in your soil.
god.huangdi.name: Huangdi
god.indra.description: Stand beneath Indra of the Vedas; the thousand-eyed king sends bolts where arrows fail.
god.indra.name: Indra
god.inti.description: Bow to Inti of the Andes; sun-temples blaze and pilgrim silver flows to your shrine.
god.inti.name: Inti
god.shango.description: Bind your forges to Shango of Yoruba thunder; iron rings when the double axe falls.
god.shango.name: Shango
god.tangaroa.description: Sail with Tangaroa of Polynesia; the sea-father claims harbors and hulls for your crown.
god.tangaroa.name: Tangaroa
wiki.factory.crossbow.manager.lore: William Tell is the Swiss rebel whose bolt split the apple on his son's head rather than bow to a tyrant's caprice. Alpine guilds still drill by his example: steady breath, clean release, and a mark that never wavers.
wiki.factory.crossbow.manager.mechanics: Appointing a manager costs roughly 220× the pillar's base unit price and automates production—you no longer need to tap. The manager stays after invocation; only gold and factory counts reset.
wiki.factory.crossbow.upgrade.lore: Trebuchets turned castle walls into negotiable rubble. Counterweight engines hurled stones—and occasionally plague—to breach towers that arrows alone could never crack.
wiki.factory.crossbow.upgrade.mechanics: Each upgrade costs about 1,000× the pillar's base unit price and permanently doubles that factory's production. Upgrades persist through god invocations.
wiki.factory.grain.manager.lore: Demeter is the Greek goddess of grain and the harvest, whose grief for Persephone once withered the earth. Eleusinian rites bound mortals to the cycle of sowing, reaping, and the law that follows a full granary.
wiki.factory.grain.manager.mechanics: Appointing a manager costs roughly 220× the pillar's base unit price and automates production—you no longer need to tap. The manager stays after invocation; only gold and factory counts reset.
wiki.factory.grain.upgrade.lore: Irrigation turned flood and drought into ledger entries. Channels, royal silos, and river treaties meant cities could eat even when the rains forgot their schedule.
wiki.factory.grain.upgrade.mechanics: Each upgrade costs about 1,000× the pillar's base unit price and permanently doubles that factory's production. Upgrades persist through god invocations.
wiki.factory.iron.manager.lore: Ilmarinen is the eternal smith of the Finnish Kalevala, who forged the Sampo and never let his anvil cool. In Idleval he shoes fields and armies alike—every ingot rings for plough and blade.
wiki.factory.iron.manager.mechanics: Appointing a manager costs roughly 220× the pillar's base unit price and automates production—you no longer need to tap. The manager stays after invocation; only gold and factory counts reset.
wiki.factory.iron.upgrade.lore: Armories turned raw iron into mail, buckles, ploughshares, and levy blades. A realm that could arm every field and every soldier stopped begging neighbors for scrap.
wiki.factory.iron.upgrade.mechanics: Each upgrade costs about 1,000× the pillar's base unit price and permanently doubles that factory's production. Upgrades persist through god invocations.
wiki.factory.longship.manager.lore: Sinbad the Sailor crossed the seven seas of Arabian legend, outwitting roc, whale, and island-sized serpent. Every hull he sends widens the crown's reach beyond any single harbor.
wiki.factory.longship.manager.mechanics: Appointing a manager costs roughly 220× the pillar's base unit price and automates production—you no longer need to tap. The manager stays after invocation; only gold and factory counts reset.
wiki.factory.longship.upgrade.lore: Colonial trade chartered companies across oceans—spice, wool, and silver flowed back while settlements took root on distant shores. Each voyage fattened the crown's purse and its map.
wiki.factory.longship.upgrade.mechanics: Each upgrade costs about 1,000× the pillar's base unit price and permanently doubles that factory's production. Upgrades persist through god invocations.
wiki.factory.wine.manager.lore: Siduri is the divine tavern keeper of the Epic of Gilgamesh, who holds the wisdom of wine at the threshold between mortal and divine. At the garden by the sea she advised the king to eat, drink, and rejoice rather than chase immortality.
wiki.factory.wine.manager.mechanics: Appointing a manager costs roughly 220× the pillar's base unit price and automates production—you no longer need to tap. The manager stays after invocation; only gold and factory counts reset.
wiki.factory.wine.upgrade.lore: The tavern league copied Siduri's garden by the sea—chartered inns at crossroads and harbors where grape became coin through hospitality. Pilgrims, merchants, and weary travelers paid for cup and bed; every stop doubled what the vine alone could earn.
wiki.factory.wine.upgrade.mechanics: Each upgrade costs about 1,000× the pillar's base unit price and permanently doubles that factory's production. Upgrades persist through god invocations.
wiki.factory.reliquary.manager.lore: Thomas Becket, Archbishop of Canterbury, was martyred in his own cathedral and became a saint whose shrine drew pilgrims from across Christendom. Wax-sealed gifts and silver awe still echo in every reliquary ledger.
wiki.factory.reliquary.manager.mechanics: Appointing a manager costs roughly 220× the pillar's base unit price and automates production—you no longer need to tap. The manager stays after invocation; only gold and factory counts reset.
wiki.factory.reliquary.upgrade.lore: Tithes codified faith into coin—every hearth paid, every child learned the creed, and pilgrim offerings swelled the treasury by law rather than by luck.
wiki.factory.reliquary.upgrade.mechanics: Each upgrade costs about 1,000× the pillar's base unit price and permanently doubles that factory's production. Upgrades persist through god invocations.
wiki.god.dagda.lore: The Dagda is the father-god of Irish myth, keeper of a cauldron that never empties and a harp that orders seasons and sorrow. Warriors and farmers alike sought his blessing at the river's edge.
wiki.god.dagda.mechanics: Requires 1 quintillion gold. Invoking grants a permanent ×3 production multiplier and resets your gold and factory counts. Multipliers from all invoked gods stack multiplicatively.
wiki.god.huangdi.lore: Huangdi—the Yellow Emperor—is the legendary founder of Chinese civilization, credited with medicine, writing, and the Mandate that ties harvest to dynasty. To invoke him is to root law and grain in the same soil.
wiki.god.huangdi.mechanics: Requires 1 trillion gold. Invoking grants a permanent ×2 production multiplier and resets your gold and factory counts. Multipliers from all invoked gods stack multiplicatively.
wiki.god.indra.lore: Indra is the Vedic king of gods, wielder of the thunderbolt and lord of storms. A thousand eyes watch the battlefield; where arrows fail, his bolt still finds the mark.
wiki.god.indra.mechanics: Requires 1 nonillion gold. Invoking grants a permanent ×5 production multiplier and resets your gold and factory counts. Multipliers from all invoked gods stack multiplicatively.
wiki.god.inti.lore: Inti is the Inca sun god, father of the first emperor and flame of the high temples. Pilgrim silver and sun-offerings flow to shrines that blaze even when the valley lies in shadow.
wiki.god.inti.mechanics: Requires 1 tredecillion gold. Invoking grants a permanent ×10 production multiplier and resets your gold and factory counts. Multipliers from all invoked gods stack multiplicatively.
wiki.god.shango.lore: Shango is the Yoruba orisha of thunder and fire, once a mortal king whose double axe still cracks the sky. Smiths bind their forges to him so iron rings true when the storm passes.
wiki.god.shango.mechanics: Requires 1 septillion gold. Invoking grants a permanent ×4 production multiplier and resets your gold and factory counts. Multipliers from all invoked gods stack multiplicatively.
wiki.god.tangaroa.lore: Tangaroa is the Polynesian sea-father who separated ocean from sky and claims every harbor and hull. Sailors bargain with him for calm water, full nets, and safe return to a wider crown.
wiki.god.tangaroa.mechanics: Requires 1 undecillion gold. Invoking grants a permanent ×8 production multiplier and resets your gold and factory counts. Multipliers from all invoked gods stack multiplicatively.
powerup.auroraDust.description: Boreal light condenses into dust, swelling all takings for a short while.
powerup.auroraDust.name: Aurora Dust
powerup.cauldronDrop.description: A drop from the endless cauldron triples your next harvest.
powerup.cauldronDrop.name: Cauldron Drop
powerup.ghostCandle.description: A flame that never consumes air keeps one workshop working alone.
powerup.ghostCandle.name: Ghost Candle
powerup.hasteRune.description: A rune that ages the world hastens every wheel in the realm.
powerup.hasteRune.name: Haste Rune
powerup.lightningShard.description: Lightning trapped in amber doubles all production briefly.
powerup.lightningShard.name: Lightning Shard
powerup.yggdrasilTear.description: Sap from the world-tree rushes every active craft forward.
powerup.yggdrasilTear.name: Yggdrasil Tear
ui.about.realmDescription: A realm does not fall for lack of gold; it falls for lack of granary, lance, and law. Raise your settlement from a single field of grain, gather tribute, and strengthen every guild and workshop, even while you ride afar.
ui.about.title: About the Realm
ui.factoryGrid.label: Factories
ui.factory.title: Factory of {0}
ui.gods.description: Surrender everything you have now, and the gods will return even greater abundance in the future.
ui.gods.title: Gods
ui.install.description: Keep the realm close and launch it from your device.
ui.install.installedDescription: Idleval is already installed on this device.
ui.install.label: Install Idleval
ui.install.title: Install Idleval
ui.install.unavailableDescription: Chrome will enable this button when the app is installable on this device.
ui.daily.description: Complete the six-day ritual to collect mystical relics.
ui.daily.powerActive: A mystical power is already active.
ui.daily.title: Daily offering
ui.inventory.description: Stack mystical relics on the altar and use them when the time is right.
ui.inventory.title: Inventory
ui.managers.description: Name legendary managers to keep each pillar running while you ride afar.
ui.managers.title: Managers
ui.nav.label: Game navigation
ui.offline.imageAlt: Zurvan, Persian god of infinite time
ui.offline.summary: You were away for {#duration}{0}{/duration} and earned {#amount}{1}{/amount}
ui.settings.subtitle: Tune courtly comforts to your liking.
ui.settings.title: Settings
ui.statistics.description: Review the ledgers of your realm's triumphs.
ui.statistics.title: Statistics
ui.upgrades.description: Build what makes the realm endure and double the crown's takings from each pillar.
ui.upgrades.title: Upgrades
ui.wiki.description: Learn the gods, legends, improvements, and counsel that keep a realm from starving.
ui.wiki.title: Royal Codex
ui.welcome.disclaimer: Idleval is a fantasy parody. No god, ruler, guild, manager, saint, monster, personality, or cosmic accountant in this game is meant to be taken seriously or treated as a real-world statement.
ui.welcome.subtitle: Raise a tiny realm, hire strange helpers, court impossible powers, and watch your coffers grow while you are away.
ui.welcome.title: Welcome to Idleval
